subject: Sports
title: India vs Pakistan: Cricket's Holy War
description: A 60-second lesson on India vs Pakistan: Cricket's Holy War.

hook.text: When India plays Pakistan in cricket, a billion people stop everything. It's the most-watched rivalry on Earth.

buildup.text: Partition in 1947 split the subcontinent into two nations. Cricket became a proxy for unresolved tensions.

discovery.text: India vs Pakistan World Cup matches regularly draw over 1 billion viewers—the biggest audiences in sport.

twist.text: The two teams rarely play bilateral series due to political tensions. Major tournaments are their only battlefield.

climax.text: The 2011 World Cup semifinal in Mohali was attended by both prime ministers as a diplomatic gesture.

punchline.text: Two nations, one game, a billion hearts racing. Cricket's fiercest rivalry.

quiz.question: Why is the India vs Pakistan cricket rivalry so intense?
options: It reflects the political tensions from the 1947 Partition
They compete for the same trophy every year
